translation: Visitors are welcome at the ashram daily from 6:00 AM to 8:00 PM. The morning satsang begins at 6:30 AM and the evening arati at 6:30 PM. First-time visitors should check in at the reception office near the main gate. Photography is permitted in the gardens but not inside the inner sanctum or the meditation hall.

Day visitors do not need to register in advance. Overnight stays require booking at least two weeks ahead via the guest house office. Our standard residential program includes vegetarian meals, a private or shared room, and access to all daily classes. Suggested contribution: 1500 INR per night for shared rooms, 2500 INR for private rooms.

We do not require any specific dress code, but we ask visitors to dress modestly: shoulders and knees covered, no sleeveless tops in the temple area. White or light-coloured clothing is traditional during festivals. Footwear must be removed before entering any temple, kitchen, or class hall.

Daily schedule: 5:00 AM wake-up bell, 5:30 AM meditation, 6:30 AM morning satsang, 7:30 AM breakfast, 9:00 AM Vedanta class, 11:00 AM Sanskrit study, 12:30 PM lunch, 2:00 PM karma yoga (seva), 4:30 PM tea, 5:00 PM bhajans, 6:30 PM evening arati, 7:30 PM dinner, 9:00 PM lights out.

Sunday is for community service and a longer satsang in the evening. The full moon (purnima) is observed with extended chanting and a silent vigil from sunset to sunrise. Major festivals (Maha Shivaratri, Guru Purnima, Navaratri, Diwali, Krishna Janmashtami) follow special schedules announced one month in advance.

Beginner Vedanta classes meet Tuesday and Thursday mornings at 9:00 AM and follow Swami Dayananda Saraswati's introduction to Tattva Bodha. Intermediate students study Vivekachudamani on Mondays and Wednesdays, and the Bhagavad Gita class runs every weekday at 10:00 AM. The classes are free of charge.

Sanskrit study is offered at three levels. Level 1 (alphabet, sandhi, basic morphology) meets MWF at 11:00 AM. Level 2 (vibhakti, dhatus, simple shlokas) meets TTh at 11:00 AM. Level 3 (Panini's Ashtadhyayi structure, Mahabhashya readings) is by invitation after at least one year at Level 2.

The ashram is supported entirely by voluntary contributions. We accept INR donations via bank transfer (account details on the contact page), UPI to ashram@oksbi, or cash at the office. International donors can use PayPal at donations@example-ashram.org. We do not solicit donations during classes and we will never pressure anyone to give. Receipts are issued for amounts above 500 INR for tax purposes.

We are a registered 80G charitable trust in India, so Indian donors can claim 50% tax deduction. International donors should consult their own tax advisors. Donations specifically earmarked for the children's free school, the kitchen, or the library are honoured per donor request. Please email accounts@example-ashram.org with the purpose if you wish to direct a gift.

We do not sell merit, mantras, initiations, or special darshans. If anyone claims to represent the ashram and asks for money outside the official channels listed on our website and these FAQs, please contact accounts@example-ashram.org so we can investigate. Sincere students who cannot afford the suggested guest contribution should write to us; we have a small fund for sponsorship.

Annual events include the ten-day Vedanta retreat (first week of January), the Sanskrit immersion (May), Guru Purnima celebrations (July full moon), Navaratri (September-October, nine nights), and the Mahasamadhi observance for our founder (date varies). The retreat schedule for the year is published on December 1 on the events page.

Online events: weekly online satsang on Sundays at 8:30 AM IST, broadcast via the ashram YouTube channel. Recordings are kept for one year. Online registration is required for live participation but the recording is public. Email media@example-ashram.org for technical issues.

General inquiries: info@example-ashram.org. Guest house and visit booking: guests@example-ashram.org. Donations and accounts: accounts@example-ashram.org. Media and press: media@example-ashram.org. Postal address: Sri Vidya Ashram, Holy Lane 12, Rishikesh 249201, Uttarakhand, India. Phone (office hours, IST): +91 135 555 0100.

For personal spiritual guidance, we recommend writing a short letter (one page or so) describing your situation, sadhana, and question. Letters are reviewed weekly by a senior teacher and replied to in turn; please allow up to three weeks. Send to guidance@example-ashram.org. Urgent welfare concerns should be marked URGENT in the subject line and we will respond within 48 hours.

We do not perform marriages, exorcisms, astrological predictions, or any ritual claiming worldly results in exchange for money. The ashram is a place of study and sadhana, not a service vendor. We do not endorse any business, political party, or product. Teachers do not give individual financial, medical, or legal advice; please consult appropriate professionals for those matters.

Our safeguarding policy: any concern of harm, harassment, or distress involving any visitor, student, or teacher should be reported immediately to the safeguarding officer at safeguarding@example-ashram.org or in person to any senior teacher. All reports are taken seriously and handled confidentially. Anyone reporting in good faith will not be retaliated against.

We do not provide mental health treatment. If you or someone you know is in crisis, please contact a qualified mental health professional or your local emergency services. India national helpline: iCall +91 9152987821. Vandrevala Foundation: 1860 2662 345. We will, of course, hold space and listen, and our teachers will respond compassionately, but professional help is essential for clinical concerns.

Our publicly available teaching archive includes recordings of Bhagavad Gita classes (Chapters 1-18), the principal Upanishads (Isha, Kena, Katha, Mundaka, Mandukya, Aitareya, Taittiriya, Brihadaranyaka, Chandogya), Brahmasutra Bhashya readings, and selected Prakarana texts (Tattva Bodha, Vivekachudamani, Drig Drishya Viveka, Aparokshanubhuti). Index: https://example-ashram.org/teachings.

Press, journalists, or researchers wishing to film or interview teachers should contact media@example-ashram.org with a written proposal at least three weeks in advance. We do not permit filming during private classes or during meditation. Photography in public areas is fine; please ask before photographing individuals.
